factions.name: Grain-Keepers
factions.description: The first faction. Farmers and shepherds who believe the harvest is the soul of the Bay. Evolves into The Grange at the bicameral split.

factions.name: Harbor League
factions.description: Merchants and watch officers who believe commerce is the Bay's lifeblood. Merges with the Threshold Guard to form the Stone Bench during the bicameral crisis.

factions.name: Threshold Guard
factions.description: Legalists and archivists who believe classification must precede action. Merges with the Harbor League to form the Stone Bench.

factions.name: The Old Way
factions.description: Pious traditionalists who believe the gods precede the Assembly. Merges with the Clerics to form the Binding Fold.

factions.name: The Binding Fold
factions.description: The merged faction of the Old Way and the Clerics. Captures the Codex Court through legal strategy. Evolves into the Docket Order.

factions.name: The Docket Order
factions.description: The legalist faction that captured the Court. Rebrands as the Federalist Union after the Imperator Compromise.

factions.name: Federalist Union
factions.description: The party of Lucian. Dominates the Imperial Era. Splinters after Lucian's death into the Lucianites, the Reformists, and the Davianites.

factions.name: The Grange
factions.description: The party of farmers and shepherds. Produces Dreson, the Cincinnatus of the hills. The conscience of the Republic.

factions.name: Judicialist League
factions.description: Corwin's breakaway faction. Argues for judicial supremacy. A permanent minority until Cassia and Darius make them contenders.

factions.name: Praetorian Order
factions.description: Not a faction but an institution. The permanent administrative and military corps. Bound constitutionally to the Imperator.

factions.name: The Silence
factions.description: The caucus that abstains. Votes only on war and constitutional crisis. Orm's legacy.
